blocks.content:
The Alert Activity page gives you a real-time view of firing and pending alert instances. You can filter, group, and drill into individual instances to investigate issues.

In this guide, you'll learn how to:
- Open the Alert Activity page
- Filter alerts by state and labels
- Group alerts and adjust the time range
- Investigate individual alert instances

## Navigate to Alert Activity

Open the Alert Activity page from the main menu.
Click **Alerting** in the main menu.
Open the **Alerts** tab.

## Understand the page layout

The page has three main areas: a filter sidebar on the left, controls at the top, and the alert workbench in the center.
The **sidebar** holds the state, severity, and label filters. Collapse or reopen it when you need more workspace.
blocks.tooltip: Toggle the filter sidebar to control your workspace.

blocks.content: The **workbench** shows alert rules on the left and state timelines on the right. The chart above it summarizes overall alert activity.
blocks.tooltip: Each row is an alert rule with its instances nested underneath.

blocks.content:
## Filter by state

The sidebar lets you quickly narrow down which alerts you see. Use the **State** filter to focus on firing or pending alerts, then clear the filter when you want the full view again.
Click **Do it** to show only firing alerts, then clear the filter to return to the full workbench.

steps.tooltip: Filters the workbench to show only instances in the firing state.
Removes the active state filter and restores the full list.

blocks.content: Click **Pending** to focus on alerts that are still waiting to fire.
blocks.tooltip: Pending alerts are evaluating as true but have not yet fired.

blocks.content: If your alerts include severity labels, use **Critical** to focus on the highest-priority instances first.
blocks.tooltip: Severity filters only appear when severity label values are present.

blocks.content:
## Use filters, group by, and time range

The controls bar at the top provides ad-hoc label filters, grouping, and a time picker. Use these to focus on specific alerts or organize the view.

blocks.title: Use filters, group by, and time range

blocks.content: Use **Filters** to add label-based filters, for example `severity = critical`.
blocks.tooltip: Supports =, !=, =~, and !~ operators for flexible label matching.

blocks.content: Use **Group by** to organize alerts by any label. Try `grafana_folder` to group alerts by folder.
blocks.tooltip: Grouped alerts nest under collapsible headers with aggregate instance counts.

blocks.content: Click the **time picker** to change the time range. Charts and instance data update to match.
blocks.tooltip: Adjusts both the summary chart and per-rule state timelines.

blocks.content:
## Investigate an alert instance

Each row in the workbench represents an alert rule. Expand a rule to see its instances, then drill into one to see query results, state transitions, and notification history.
Alert rule rows include **Rule details**. Use that marker to pick a rule row before you expand it.
blocks.tooltip: This helps you distinguish alert rule rows from higher-level group rows.

blocks.content: Expand one alert rule to reveal its instances. When you can see an **Instance details** action in that expanded list, click **Continue**.
Start the guided walkthrough to open **Instance details** and inspect one alert instance. The drawer covers the guide, so follow the tooltips until it closes.

steps.description: Click **Instance details** to open the drawer.
Hover here to inspect the query visualization, including the evaluated metric data and thresholds.
Hover here to inspect the **History** filters. Use them to switch between **All**, **State changes**, and **Notifications**.
Click a notification event to inspect its delivery details.
Click the close control to return to the Alert Activity workbench.

blocks.content:
Use **Instance details** to answer three questions:

- **What data triggered this instance?** Check the query visualization and thresholds.
- **How did the state change over time?** Use **History** to review transitions.
- **Who was notified?** Open a notification event to inspect delivery details.

You now know how to:

- Navigate to the Alert Activity page
- Filter by state and labels using the sidebar
- Use ad-hoc filters and group by to organize the view
- Expand rules to see instances and their state timelines
- Open the instance details drawer for investigation
